content:
What are Technology Readiness Levels (TRL 1-3)?

TRL 1: Basic Principles Observed

Scientific research begins to be translated into applied research and development. Examples include basic properties of materials or algorithms.

TRL 2: Technology Concept Formulated

Practical applications can be invented. The application is speculative, and there may be no proof or detailed analysis to support the assumptions.

TRL 3: Analytical & Experimental Critical Function Proof

Active R&D is initiated. This includes analytical studies and laboratory studies to physically validate the analytical predictions of separate elements of the technology.

What Programs Support Early-Stage Ideation?

NSERC Discovery Grants Program

  • Natural sciences and engineering
  • Fundamental research programs
  • Long-term research objectives
  • Novel concepts and methodologies
  • 5-year funding cycles
  • Research independence and flexibility
  • Student training support
  • Equipment and infrastructure funding

SSHRC Insight Grants - Social Sciences Research

  • Social sciences and humanities
  • Arts and creative research
  • Policy and governance studies
  • Cultural and societal research
  • Research assistant funding
  • Travel and fieldwork support
  • Knowledge mobilization
  • Dissemination activities

Mitacs Accelerate - University-Industry Research

  • Applied research problems
  • Technology exploration
  • Feasibility studies
  • Market research and analysis
  • Cost-share model (50/50)
  • Access to university talent
  • Flexible project duration
  • Multiple internships possible

CIHR Project Grants - Health Research

  • Biomedical research
  • Clinical research
  • Health services research
  • Population health studies
  • Flexible project duration
  • Open competition format
  • Peer review process
  • Knowledge translation support

Case Study: How Does Early-Stage Funding Work in Practice?

BioPolymer Solutions (Hypothetical)

University spin-out developing biodegradable plastics from algae.

Professor secured $40,000 NSERC grant to theorize algae strain modification formula.

Partnered with local manufacturing firm to fund a PhD student internship ($30k) for formulation testing.

IRAP provided $50,000 to validate the degradation rate in a controlled lab environment. IP filed.

What are Common Questions About Ideation & Research Funding?

Quick answers to the most frequently asked questions about early-stage research funding in Canada.

Who can apply for NSERC Discovery Grants?

Researchers at eligible Canadian universities conducting fundamental research

What does TRL 1-3 mean?

Early discovery phase: basic research, concept formulation, and proof-of-principle

How does Mitacs Accelerate work?

Cost-shared internships connecting businesses with graduate researchers

How much funding is available?

Up to $1M through NSERC Discovery, $400K through SSHRC, $750K through CIHR

How long is the NSERC application process?

6-8 months from Notice of Intent to approval (August to April cycle)

What are acceptable Stage 1 outcomes?

Publications, proof-of-concept, IP foundations, and Stage 2 readiness

📚 Related Guides

Stage 2: Development & Proof-of-Concept

Move from research to prototype with IRAP and SR&ED funding

Stage 3: Demonstration & Pilot

Scale to pre-commercial demonstration with SDTC and Clean Growth

Funding available: up to $1M.
shortAnswerQuestion: How can my business apply for Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada 2026 in 2026?

faq.question: What is the application deadline for Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada  | NSERC Discovery Grants | Up to  Early-Stage R&D in 2026?
faq.answer: Deadlines typically vary by specific program tier. However, for 2026, many initiatives operate on a rolling intake basis until funds are depleted. Check the official portal for specific cutoffs.

faq.question: How much funding can I get through Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada  | NSERC Discovery Grants | Up to  Early-Stage R&D?
faq.answer: Funding amounts range depending on business size and scope. Top awards can reach $1M.

faq.question: Do I have to give up equity for this program?
faq.answer: No. Government grants and subsidies are non-dilutive, meaning you retain 100% ownership of your company.

expertTip.title: Academic Partners
expertTip.content: Partner with a university researcher. NSERC grants are often more accessible when you have an academic collaboration.
